course_title: Human Function in Health & Disease B

learning_outcomes.description: Knowledge - Explain how physiological dysfunctions can result in stress and disease including obesity, diabetes, and disorders of movement.
Knowledge - Demonstrate an understanding of how physiological dysfunctions can result in stress and diseases including obesity, diabetes and disorders of movement.
Knowledge - Evaluate how disease states can impact on daily living, and how occupational therapists can communicate their role.
Knowledge - Test the assertions made in lectures and textbooks and to establish the conditions under which these assertions might or might not apply.
Practicality - Integrate laboratory data with the theory of physiology.
Metacognition - Reflect effectively on your learning in physiology.

assessments.task_description:
This exam will contain a variety of questions, including but not limited to, short answer and multiple-choice questions. Content covered in the exam will include lectures in Weeks 1-5 inclusive and the Parkinson's disease practical.

This exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera.

Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. 

You must bring your current UQ student ID card and present it on entry to the examination. If you do not have your UQ student ID card with you on the day of examination, Examinations Procedure clause 28 outlines acceptable student identification that may be presented at examinations. Students unable to produce the acceptable forms of identification (i.e. their current UQ student identification card or a document issued by the Student Centre) are NOT permitted to enter the examination room.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
An Information Sheet aimed at a multi-disciplinary team describing a disease covered in the course and the impact of the disease on occupational performance. Word length = 1200 words.

More details about this assessment will be provided during the semester via classes and Blackboard.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Students are permitted to recycle previous work as approved by the Course Coordinator for this assessment. Please review the Student Code of Conduct Policy for further information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet available on the School's website (under Undergraduate - Assessment), to Turnitin by the submission deadline. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 2(27 Jul - 09 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Movement and Posture

Mary-Louise Roy Manchadi and Shyuan Ngo

4hrs of independent study lectures (Brain control of movement and posture, Parkinson's and Huntington's diseases, other movement disorders), plus a live on campus activity (15min) for course introduction in week 1 (Shyuan Ngo), and live on campus activity movement lectorial in week 2.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Movement and Posture Mary-Louise Roy Manchadi and Shyuan Ngo 4hrs of independent study lectures (Brain control of movement and posture, Parkinson's and Huntington's diseases, other movement disorders), plus a live on campus activity (15min) for course introduction in week 1 (Shyuan Ngo), and live on campus activity movement lectorial in week 2.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 6(10 Aug - 06 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 2: Endocrinology

Shyuan Ngo and Louise Ainscough

Shyuan Ngo - 4hrs of lectures on campus (Introduction to the Endocrine System; Thyroid Function and Metabolism; Stress)

Louise Ainscough - 4hrs of independent study lectures (Diabetes and Obesity; Growth, Bones and Ageing).
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2: Endocrinology Shyuan Ngo and Louise Ainscough Shyuan Ngo - 4hrs of lectures on campus (Introduction to the Endocrine System; Thyroid Function and Metabolism; Stress) Louise Ainscough - 4hrs of independent study lectures (Diabetes and Obesity; Growth, Bones and Ageing).

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 4(10 Aug - 23 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Reflexes and balance

In this practical, you will test your iris reflexes, stretch reflexes, ulnar nerve conduction velocity, balance and motor control. Practical group 1 will attend in week 3, and group 2 will attend in week 4.

PPE required: Closed in shoes. You do not need your lab coat or safety glasses.
learning_activities.topic_description: Reflexes and balance In this practical, you will test your iris reflexes, stretch reflexes, ulnar nerve conduction velocity, balance and motor control. Practical group 1 will attend in week 3, and group 2 will attend in week 4. PPE required: Closed in shoes. You do not need your lab coat or safety glasses.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 5 To Week 6(24 Aug - 06 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Oral glucose tolerance test

In this practical, you will be measuring your blood glucose levels after taking a glucose load. This practical works best if you fast in the morning before the test. Practical group 1 will attend in week 5, and group 2 will attend in week 6.

PPE required: You will need your laboratory coat, safety glasses and closed in shoes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Oral glucose tolerance test In this practical, you will be measuring your blood glucose levels after taking a glucose load. This practical works best if you fast in the morning before the test. Practical group 1 will attend in week 5, and group 2 will attend in week 6. PPE required: You will need your laboratory coat, safety glasses and closed in shoes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 8 To Week 11(14 Sep - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 3: Wound healing, Burns, Pain and Inflammation

Louise Ainscough

4hrs of independent study lectures (Skin; Wound healing and Burns; Pain), plus a lectorial on campus in week 11.
learning_activities.topic_title: Module 3: Wound healing, Burns, Pain and Inflammation
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 3: Wound healing, Burns, Pain and Inflammation Louise Ainscough 4hrs of independent study lectures (Skin; Wound healing and Burns; Pain), plus a lectorial on campus in week 11.

Assignment Information Session

Louise Ainscough

In this on campus lecture we will go through the requirements of the information sheet assignment. This is your chance to ask questions!

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 10 To Week 13(05 Oct - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 4: Renal system and Ageing

Louise Ainscough

6hrs of independent study lectures (Renal physiology; Dehydration; Chronic kidney disease; Urinary incontinence), plus a lectorial on campus in week 13.
